person.name: Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont

nameForms.fullText: Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Lord Thomas de Moulton (21 February 1276-26 November 1313) was the first Baron Multon of Gilsland.

He was the son of Thomas de Moulton of Egremont, Cumberland. He married Eleanor de Burgh, daughter of Richard de Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster; Richard's other daughter, Elizabeth de Burgh, married King Robert the Bruce of Scotland.

On 26 August 1307, Thomas de Moulton was summoned to Parliament as Baron Moulton of Gilsland, the only creation of that title in the Peerage of England. He was engaged in many of the Scottish wars and subsequently obtained many immunities from the crown in the shape of grants for fairs and markets upon his many manors.

He died on 26 November 1313 leaving an only daughter and heiress, Margaret, who inherited the title and estates and was later nicknamed the "Flower of Gillesland". She married Ranulph (Ralph) de Dacre, whom she married because she found him chivalrous. Her father originally opposed the match, but relented when he discovered that the Dacre family was equal to his own in wealth and power, according to the Moulton Annals. Ranulph was later summoned to Parliament as Lord Dacre in 1321. The Multon title and estates were inherited by the Dacre family after Margaret's death in 1361.

Thomas was the great-great-grandson of Thomas de Moulton (died 1240).

He did homage & had livery of his mother's lands before he came of age, 12 May 1296. He probably accompanied the Bishop of Ely on a mission to the Court of Guelders, Jan. 1295/6. Summoned to Parliament whereby he is held to have become Lord Multon, 6 Feb. 1298/9. Served in Scotland & on the border between 1297 & 1315 being one of the northern barons chiefly concerned with defense against the Scots. He took part in the Siege of Carlaverock, 1300. His seal was appended to the Barons' letter to the Pope, Feb. 1300/1. In 1305 he went to Ireland with a large retinue & was again in Scotland in 1306. Summoned to the coronation of King Edward II, Jan. 1307/8. However, he later rebelled & joined Thomas, Earl of Lancaster at Pontefract, May 1321, but died before the Battle of Boroughbridge which was lost by the rebels. Died 1321/2.

It is uncertain whether it was he or his namesake nephew of Egremont who was appointed Warden of the Coast and keeper of Carlisle Castle.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont於1276年2月21日出生在英國波藍德時，他的父親Thomas II de Multon of Egremont27歲，母親Edmunda Boteler22歲。他於1297年1月3日與Eleanor de Burgh在英國素福爾可易普斯維其結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1322年2月8日之前在英國越可爾波洛賀布利其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont于1276年2月21日出生在英国波藍德时，他的父亲Thomas II de Multon of Egremont27岁，母亲Edmunda Boteler22岁。他于1297年1月3日与Eleanor de Burgh在英国素福爾可易普斯維其结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1322年2月8日之前在英国越可爾波洛賀布利其。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Multon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Moulton
meanings: English (mainly Cheshire and Lancashire): habitational name from one or more of the places called Moulton (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Northamptonshire, Staffordshire, Suffolk, North Yorkshire), most of which are named with the Old English personal name Mūl(a), the Old Norse personal name Múli, or Old English mūl ‘mule’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont was born on 21 February 1276, in Cumberland, England, his father, Thomas II de Multon of Egremont, was 27 and his mother, Edmunda Boteler, was 22. He married Eleanor de Burgh on 3 January 1297, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died before 8 February 1322, in Boroughbridge, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont est né le 21 février 1276, à Cumberland, Angleterre, son père, Thomas II de Multon of Egremont, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Edmunda Boteler, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor de Burgh le 3 janvier 1297, à Ipswich, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé avant 8 février 1322, à Boroughbridge, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont am 21. Februar 1276 in Cumberland, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas II de Multon of Egremont 27 und seine Mutter Edmunda Boteler 22. Er hat am 3. Januar 1297 in Ipswich, Suffolk, England Eleanor de Burgh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb vor 8. Februar 1322 in Boroughbridge, Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont è nato il il 21 febbraio 1276 nel Cumberland, England, il suo padre, Thomas II de Multon of Egremont, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Edmunda Boteler, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Eleanor de Burgh il 3 gennaio 1297, nel Ipswich, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì prima del 8 febbraio 1322, nel Boroughbridge, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremontが1276年2月21日にイギリスクウンバーランドで生まれた時，父Thomas II de Multon of Egremontは27歳，母Edmunda Botelerは22歳であった。1297年1月3日にイギリススフォルクイプスウィチでEleanor de Burghと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1322年2月8日以前にイギリスヨークシュアイアボロウブリッジで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont(이)가 1276년 2월 21일 영국 쿠벌랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas II de Multon of Egremont(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Edmunda Boteler(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1297년 1월 3일 영국 수폴크 이프스위치에서 Eleanor de Burgh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1322년 2월 8일 이전 영국 요르크실르 버루브리지에서 사망했다.

mn.21 February 1276–before 8 February 1322: 1276 оны Хоёрдугаар сарын 21–1322 оны Хоёрдугаар сарын 8–наас өмнө

pt.21 February 1276–before 8 February 1322: 21 de fevereiro de 1276–antes 8 de fevereiro de 1322
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont nasceu em 21 de fevereiro de 1276, em Cumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas II de Multon of Egremont, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Edmunda Boteler, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor de Burgh em 3 de janeiro de 1297, em Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 8 de fevereiro de 1322, em Boroughbridge, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont родился 21 февраля 1276 г. в местности кумбирланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas II de Multon of Egremont было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Edmunda Boteler, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor de Burgh 3 января 1297 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер перед 8 февраль 1322 г. в местности бороугбридж, еркшир, Англия.

es.21 February 1276–before 8 February 1322: 21 de febrero de 1276–antes de 8 de febrero de 1322
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont nació el 21 de febrero de 1276, en Cumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas II de Multon of Egremont, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Edmunda Boteler, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Eleanor de Burgh el 3 de enero de 1297, en Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió antes de 8 de febrero de 1322, en Boroughbridge, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
